etimesYour old laptops might hide Gold! Swiss scientists pull pure Gold from scrapped computer parts using 'cheese waste'Researchers in Switzerland have developed an innovative method to extract pure gold from electronic waste using whey, a byproduct of cheese production. This eco-friendly process utilizes protein "sponges" to selectively capture gold ions from discarded computer parts, offering a sustainable and cost-effective alternative to traditional recycling methods.

etimes“It hurts to close kitchens…”: Delhi restaurant shuts amid LPG crisis; owner claims ₹5,000 black market rateFood businesses across India face disruption due to an ongoing LPG shortage. In South Delhi, the North Indian restaurant Tadka Rani has temporarily ceased operations. Owner Gagandeep Singh Sapra cited the unavailability of LPG cylinders and exorbitant black market prices. He expressed distress over closing kitchens and taking the restaurant offline from delivery platforms.

etimesAre LPG delivery workers earning ₹30,000 a month? Viral post sparks debateA Varanasi investor's calculation of LPG delivery workers' daily earnings sparked online debate. While he estimated around Rs 1,000 daily based on Rs 25 per cylinder for 30-40 deliveries, social media users countered, highlighting the immense physical labor, long hours, and rising fuel costs involved. Many emphasized that the compensation is insufficient for such demanding work.
